message.content:
Record the NEEDS-ALIAS renames. Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2, PR #1384 exists to base gate-inversion).
Only commit if validate gates passed (collection_ok=true, oracle_matches=true, aliases_all_resolve=true) and codex=ALIASES-SAFE (ALIASES-REVISE). Else commit nothing, report blocker.
1. Stage only src/ + scripts/ + tests/ changes (NOT the overlaid tests/oracle/*). Grep-check no secret.
2. Append to docs/review_readiness/phase2_renames_applied.md: the NEEDS-ALIAS batch — applied count, aliases added, skipped (source-grep/conflicting) for human review, gates passed.
3. Commit ("Phase 2: NEEDS-ALIAS renames with backward-compat aliases (score-safe, oracle-validated)") with trailers:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TLpJSqNfJVSP1UGdtk89nA
4. Push (updates PR #1384): 'cd /home/polaris/wt/phase2 && git push'.
Return {commit_sha, pushed, committed_or_blocked}.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The acceptance_result.json is unstaged (modified). Let me read the phase2_renames_applied.md doc and look for the validation gates and codex verdict. The task says to only commit if specific gates passed. Let me find where those gate results are recorded.

content.content:
1	# Phase 2B — SAFE-static + TEXT-ONLY renames (applied)
2	
3	**Branch:** `chore/review-readiness-phase2` · **Base for renames:** `c5448ec` (Phase 1C head)
4	**Scope:** the two lowest-risk classes from the codex-validated Phase 2A public-compat
5	worklist — score-safe, non-public-contract identifier renames only.
6	
7	## Source of truth
8	
9	- Worklist: `NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv` (346 rows; 210 `RENAME`, 136 `KEEP*`).
10	- Per-row disposition (§6.5 of `public_compat_inventory.md`), codex-verified `2A: OK`:
11	  `SAFE-static=100, TEXT-ONLY=12, FILE-RENAME=45, NEEDS-ALIAS=27, DYNAMIC-HAZARD=10,
12	  DOMAIN-REVIEW=16` (sums to 210, exact integers, no approximations).
13	
14	Phase 2B applies **only** the `SAFE-static` and `TEXT-ONLY` rows. The remaining four
15	classes (FILE-RENAME, NEEDS-ALIAS, DYNAMIC-HAZARD, DOMAIN-REVIEW) are deliberately **out
16	of scope** here — they touch import surfaces, env-var / persisted-string control surfaces,
17	dynamic name derivation, or need domain review, and require alias/migration handling, not a
18	naive rename.
19	
20	## Classes applied
21	
22	| Class | Candidates | Applied | Skipped / noted | What it is |
23	|-------|-----------:|--------:|----------------:|------------|
24	| SAFE-static | 105 | 102 | 3 | Local vars, `_`-prefixed module-private helpers, symbols behind FastAPI path strings, str-Enum member names (values unchanged) — no external import surface, serialize nothing. |
25	| TEXT-ONLY | 12 | 10 | 2 | Cosmetic identifier fixes (typos, non-descriptive names) with no cross-module reference. |
26	| **Total** | **117** | **112** | **5** | |
27	
28	### Skipped / noted (5) — deliberately not mechanically renamed
29	
30	- `OpenAIShimClient` @ `scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/pg_compose_openai_validation.py:58` —
31	  retired script, no target new name.
32	- `_BANKED_RUN` @ `scripts/iarch011_b11_compose_repetition_harness.py:35` — better modelled
33	  as a configurable `BANKED_RUN_DIR`, a behavior change, not a pure rename.
34	- `contracts_v3` @ `src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:14` — submodule-name decision
35	  deferred (naming choice + import-surface review).
36	- `build_and_run_v4` @ `scripts/live_server.py:557` and
37	  @ `src/polaris_graph/pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:187` — drop the `_v4` version suffix only
38	  after the dead alternate branches are removed (sequencing dependency), not now.
39	
40	## Files changed
41	
42	74 files, 560 insertions / 560 deletions — a symmetric per-reference rename diff
43	(each renamed occurrence is one deletion + one addition; no whole-file churn):
44	
45	- `src/` — 28 files
46	- `scripts/` — 28 files
47	- `tests/` — 18 files (rename call-site updates only; e.g.
48	  `create_v3_state` → `create_lightweight_state` in `tests/v3/test_graph.py`)
49	
50	Explicitly **excluded from this commit** (not renames): the overlaid deterministic-oracle
51	tooling used only to *run* the gate — `tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py`,
52	`tests/oracle/retrieval_cassette.py`, `tests/oracle/cassettes/*`, and local drift in
53	`tests/oracle/cassette.py` / `tests/oracle/llm_cassette.py` — plus the oracle run artifact
54	`acceptance_result.json`. None of these carry a worklist rename token.
55	
56	## Validation gates (all passed)
57	
58	1. **Collection gate — `collection_ok = true`.**
59	   `pytest --collect-only` on the working tree (renames applied) collects **16,738 tests
60	   with 11 collection errors**, byte-for-byte the same as `HEAD` with the renames stashed
61	   (**16,738 tests / 11 errors**). The 11 errors are the pre-existing baseline (registry
62	   import-time filesystem validation + playwright browser), unchanged by the renames — zero
63	   new import breakage.
64	
65	2. **Config characterization — green.** The Phase 1C config characterization suite
66	   (`resolve == os.getenv` over all keys; collection-stable baseline) remains green on this
67	   branch; renames touch only identifier names, not config keys or defaults.
68	
69	3. **Oracle replay — `oracle_matches = true`.**
70	   `acceptance_portable.py --replay` (frozen LLM + retrieval cassettes, zero network) with
71	   the renames applied reproduces the golden **byte-identical**:
72	   - GOLDEN SHA-256: `9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98`
73	     (3,184 bytes) — `replay artifact BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden`.
74	   - Positive control (THIN): `valid_positive_control = true` (search fired, outline mutated).
75	   - Negative control (SATURATED): `valid_negative_control = true` (zero searches, full loop,
76	     `finish_outline` accepted at turn 1).
77	   - `ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid`.
78	
79	## Codex verdict — `RENAMES-SAFE`
80	
81	Codex (GPT-5.6) reviewed the applied batch and returned **`RENAMES-SAFE`**. Key points:
82	
83	- Known dynamic / ambiguous cases were excluded up front; static references were mechanically
84	  and completely updated; collection behavior did not regress; characterization stayed green;
85	  the covered oracle path is byte-identical.
86	- For repository-wide whole-token replacement, ordinary in-repo Python references are
87	  reference-complete — a static identifier cannot hide from a correctly scoped token search.
88	- Residual risk is nonzero but confined to **undiscovered dynamic, name-derived, external, or
89	  unexecuted contracts** (constructed strings, `getattr`/registry reflection, `__name__`-derived
90	  contracts, generated/non-Python assets, downstream consumers) — **not** ordinary
91	  in-repository static references. "Reference-complete" is repository-static completeness, not
92	  proof against every runtime or external reference.
93	- Non-blocking follow-up suggested: a final raw grep of every applied old token across all
94	  tracked file types, and matching collected node-IDs (not just error counts) in the baseline
95	  comparison.
96	
97	## Bottom line
98	
99	All three gate preconditions hold — `collection_ok = true`, `oracle_matches = true`, codex
100	verdict `RENAMES-SAFE`. The 112 applied renames are the score-safe, oracle-validated subset;
101	the higher-risk classes remain deferred to later, alias-aware phases.
102

content.text:
The gate/codex artifacts aren't obviously present as files. The critical requirement is: I must have evidence that (collection_ok=true, oracle_matches=true, aliases_all_resolve=true) AND codex=ALIASES-SAFE. Without this evidence, the instruction is explicit: "commit nothing, report blocker."

+# Backward-compat alias: old serialized mode_label value that already-migrated

+LEGACY_MODE_LABEL_LEGACY_ALIAS = "Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild"
+MIGRATION_MARKER = "_pre_rebuild_migration"
+# Backward-compat: old migration-marker key written into pre-existing run
+# artifacts. Idempotency detection MUST still recognize this old key, otherwise
+# already-migrated runs would be re-migrated. Do not remove.
+MIGRATION_MARKER_LEGACY_ALIAS = "_honest_rebuild_migration"

-    """Check if the file has already been migrated (idempotency guard)."""

+    runs migrated under the old contract are not re-migrated.
+    """
+    return (
+        data.get(MIGRATION_MARKER) is not None
+        or data.get(MIGRATION_MARKER_LEGACY_ALIAS) is not None
+    )
